bnf.brief.p1: For the reopening of the Richelieu site after its renovation, the Bibliothèque nationale de France wanted visitors to explore collection highlights beyond the vitrines — the stories and details a label can't hold.
bnf.brief.p2: The experience had to welcome every kind of visitor: from art historians to first-time visitors, from French speakers to international guests, exploring alone or gathered in a small group around the same screen.
bnf.built.p1: I worked on the creative development and front-end of the experience — translating the graphic intent into a touch-driven interface, from integration through interactions and animation.
bnf.built.p2: Visitors explore grouped collection highlights — including ancient coins — by touch, then zoom into individual works for a closer look. A multi-language idle attract screen (DÉBUT · START · INICIO) invites passers-by without a word of instruction.
bnf.detail.fig-a.label: FIG. A — COIN CLEANING
bnf.detail.fig-b.label: FIG. B — ILLUSTRATED NARRATIVE
bnf.detail.fig-c.label: FIG. C — MULTI-LANGUAGE INTERFACE
bnf.detail.note-1.desc: Artworks, maps and stories share one continuous space — smooth transitions keep visitors oriented as they move between scales, from the full collection down to a single detail.
bnf.detail.note-1.title: Fluid navigation between works
bnf.detail.note-2.desc: A multi-language idle attract screen demonstrates the experience before anyone touches it, so the first interaction teaches the rest.
bnf.detail.note-2.title: Zero-instruction interface
bnf.engineering.experience.desc: An idle attract loop invites passers-by to start exploring without a word of instruction.
bnf.engineering.experience.label: EXPERIENCE
bnf.engineering.interaction.desc: Touch-first gestures designed for a shared, large-format screen — generous hit targets that work for one visitor or several at once.
bnf.engineering.interaction.label: INTERACTION
bnf.engineering.intro: A few notes on how the experience was built for a shared, public touch screen.
bnf.engineering.languages.desc: French, English and Spanish content switching, shown in the attract screen (DÉBUT · START · INICIO).
bnf.engineering.languages.label: LANGUAGES
bnf.engineering.stack.desc: Specific technologies for this production are still to be confirmed.
bnf.engineering.stack.label: STACK

carbon.hero.description: This site aims to minimize environmental impact through efficient code, optimized images, and reduced motion rendering.

carbon.strategies.title: Optimization Strategies
carbon.strategy.1: Lazy loading and code splitting for animations and interactive elements.
carbon.strategy.2: Optimized vector graphics and compressed assets for minimal bandwidth usage.
carbon.strategy.3: Server-side rendering and intelligent caching strategies.
carbon.strategy.4: Efficient CSS with Tailwind's purge system to reduce bundle size.
carbon.strategy.5: Modern image formats (WebP, AVIF) with fallbacks for compatibility.
fdp.approach.01.desc: Custom interaction models and canvas work where they serve the story — never as decoration.
fdp.approach.01.title: Creative development
fdp.approach.02.desc: Public installations must work for everyone: children, seniors and first-time visitors alike.
fdp.approach.02.title: Accessibility
fdp.approach.03.desc: Smooth motion on modest kiosk hardware and fast loads on gallery networks — performance is part of the design.
fdp.approach.03.title: Performance
fdp.approach.04.desc: Motion as narration: GSAP timelines and scroll choreography that guide attention rather than distract.
fdp.approach.04.title: Animation & motion
fdp.approach.05.desc: Editorial structures that turn collections and archives into narratives visitors want to follow.
fdp.approach.05.title: Interactive storytelling
fdp.approach.06.desc: Migrating legacy Flash / ActionScript 3 experiences to standards-based JavaScript and Vue.js.
fdp.approach.06.title: Flash → modern web
fdp.context.mission.heading: The mission
fdp.context.mission.p1: Fleur de Papier crafts digital mediation for the cultural sector — turning collections, archives and exhibitions into experiences the public can touch, play and explore, for national institutions, regional museums and cultural venues.
fdp.context.mission.p2: Every production balances curatorial rigor with playfulness: content shaped together with curators and institutions, interfaces designed for visitors of every age, running on everything from gallery touch tables to exhibition kiosks.
fdp.context.role.heading: My role
fdp.context.role.p1: I worked as creative developer and front-end developer — building the interaction and animation layers, translating design intent into working interfaces, and taking productions from prototype to on-site installation alongside designers, project managers and illustrators.
fdp.hero.intro.body: . As creative developer, I built immersive, educational web experiences for cultural institutions — interfaces for the web, touch screens and exhibition contexts — working alongside designers, project managers, illustrators and the institutions themselves.
fdp.hero.title: Fleur de Papier

productions.bnf-richelieu.description: An interactive digital experience for the Richelieu site of the Bibliothèque nationale de France. Visitors explore grouped collection items — including ancient coins — by touch, zoom into individual works for a closer look, and are welcomed by a multi-language idle attract screen (DÉBUT · START · INICIO).

testimonials.1.body.1: I managed Rama at Ansys while he was part of our platform engineering team.
testimonials.1.body.2: Rama is a skilled Front-End / Design Engineer with strong attention to detail and a genuine sense for user experience. He consistently delivered clean, well-structured front-end code and contributed to building intuitive and reliable internal tools.
testimonials.1.body.3: His understanding extends beyond the user interface. Rama worked on full-stack tooling used by more than 200 engineers to manage Kubernetes deployments, combining Angular frontends with CI/CD pipelines built with GitHub Actions. This gave him a solid understanding of platform engineering constraints and of how front-end applications integrate into broader infrastructure and deployment workflows.
testimonials.1.body.4: He is proactive, curious, and always willing to explore better solutions. Rama also contributes positively to the team environment and is great to collaborate with.
testimonials.1.body.5: I would gladly recommend him to any team looking for a thoughtful, versatile and reliable engineer.
testimonials.1.excerpt: Rama is a skilled Front-End / Design Engineer with strong attention to detail and a genuine sense for user experience. He worked on full-stack tooling used by more than 200 engineers to manage Kubernetes deployments, combining Angular frontends with CI/CD pipelines.
testimonials.1.name: Mathilde Gallouët
testimonials.2.body.1: I worked with Rama at Fleur de Papier, where he quickly stood out as a creative and highly reliable developer.
testimonials.2.body.2: Rama played a key role in the transition of our interactive projects from Adobe Flash (ActionScript) to modern web technologies (JavaScript / Vue), bringing both technical initiative and a strong sensitivity to design and user experience.
testimonials.2.body.3: He was not only focused on code quality but also contributed creatively by proposing animations and interactions that enhanced the immersive experiences we built for cultural institutions such as the BnF, museums and exhibitions.
testimonials.2.body.4: Rama is proactive, curious and always eager to improve both the technical and creative aspects of a project. He is also very easy to work with and brings positive energy to a team. I would gladly recommend him to any team looking for a thoughtful and creative front-end engineer.
testimonials.2.designation: Fleur de Papier — Digital Cultural Mediation
testimonials.2.excerpt: Rama played a key role in the transition of our interactive projects from Adobe Flash to modern web technologies (JavaScript / Vue). He contributed creatively by proposing animations and interactions that enhanced the immersive experiences we built for the BnF, museums and exhibitions.
testimonials.2.name: Romain Déflache
testimonials.3.body.1: I had the opportunity to work with Rama at Ansys where he was working as a Front-end / UI Engineer.
testimonials.3.body.2: Rama is a talented engineer with strong front-end skills and a great sense of UI/UX. He consistently delivers clean, maintainable interfaces and approaches problems with both technical rigor and creativity.
testimonials.3.body.3: He is also very collaborative and great to work with. Rama is a passionate, skilled and creative engineer. I would happily recommend him to any team building modern web applications.
testimonials.3.excerpt: Rama is a talented engineer with strong front-end skills and a great sense of UI/UX. He consistently delivers clean, maintainable interfaces and approaches problems with both technical rigor and creativity. He is also very collaborative and great to work with.
testimonials.3.name: Hichem Rekouane
testimonials.4.body.1: I had the pleasure of working with Rama on our final-year project at Gobelins, and can attest to his professionalism and his front-end skills. Our collaboration was smooth and efficient.
testimonials.4.excerpt: I had the pleasure of working with Rama on our final-year project at Gobelins, and can attest to his professionalism and his front-end skills. Our collaboration was smooth and efficient.
testimonials.4.name: Elie Nissen
